nameMeanings.name: Hackney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Hackney in Greater London, named from an Old English personal name Haca (genitive Hacan) + ēg ‘island, dry ground in marshland’.

perhaps from Middle English hakenei (Old French haquenée), an ambling horse or mare, especially one considered suitable for women to ride; perhaps therefore a metonymic occupational name for a breeder or keeper of such horses and short for Hakeneyman. This surname has also been found in Scotland since medieval times.

variants.name: Oakley
meanings: English (mainly West Midlands): habitational name from any of numerous places called Oakley, Oakle, or Oakleigh, in Bedfordshire, Essex, Gloucestershire, Hampshire, Kent, Somerset, Suffolk, or Wiltshire. The placenames derive from Old English āc ‘oak’ + lēah ‘open woodland’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Hackney was born in 1485, in Horsham, Sussex, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 daughter with Mrs Thomas Hackney. He died after 1502, in his hometown, and was buried in St Mary's Church, Barton upon Humber, Lincolnshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Hackneyは1485年にイギリスイギリススセックスホールシャムで生まれた。彼はMrs Thomas Hackneyとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1502年以後に故郷で死去し，イギリスリンコルンシュアイアバートン ウプオン ハンバースト マリ'ス チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Hackney(은)는 1485년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 호르샤에서 태어났다. 그는 Mrs Thomas Hackney(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1502년 이후 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 링코실르 바턴 업 훔버 스트 마이'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.
